waits for a bus that stopped running an hour ago
searches a coat pocket for something that isn't there
writes the same sentence over and over in a notebook
watches the door without moving toward it
counts the money again
folds the same piece of paper until it tears
puts on lipstick in a broken mirror
carries a box of photographs to a waiting car
stands at the window watching the street go dark
reads a menu in a restaurant that's about to close
steps off a train and looks around as if someone might be there
dials a number and hangs up before it connects
unpacks a suitcase into an unfamiliar room
stares at a meal they're not going to eat
lights a candle and then blows it out immediately
walks into a room that used to be different
reads the departure board one more time
sits in a car in the driveway, not going inside
stands on a bridge without looking down
leaves through the back exit
writes a name on the foggy window
holds a phone that isn't ringing
folds a shirt that belongs to someone else
drinks alone but isn't drunk
looks at a photograph for what may be the last time
walks the same route for the hundredth time
tries on a coat that doesn't fit
empties a desk drawer into a plastic bag
stops in the middle of the street for no visible reason
writes a note and leaves it somewhere they know it will be found
tears something carefully so it doesn't look torn
closes a door quietly so no one hears
irons a shirt that has already been ironed
throws away a photograph and then picks it up again
reads a letter standing on the platform before boarding
sits in someone else's chair without knowing it
washes dishes that were already clean
says goodbye to a room before leaving it
buys a ticket for a destination they've been avoiding
falls asleep somewhere they meant to leave an hour ago
traces a crack in the wall with one finger
rehearses a sentence they'll never say out loud
stares at the ceiling in a room with one overhead bulb
locks a door and then checks it three times
burns a letter they should have sent
pours a drink and doesn't touch it
sits on the floor beside the bed at 3am
watches someone from across the street without moving
turns off a light and stands in the dark for a moment
opens a drawer, closes it without taking anything

pretends not to notice the person who just walked in
puts on a record and stands very still
flinches at a sound that turns out to be nothing
signs a document without reading it
packs a bag and then unpacks it again
waits outside a hospital exit in the cold
leaves food on a table for someone who isn't coming back
buttons a jacket with hands that won't stop shaking
answers a question with a pause that says everything
looks at a clock and then looks away before reading it
steps outside in the rain without a coat
wipes down a counter that's already clean
slides a key under a door and walks away
picks up a child's toy and holds it longer than necessary
nods at something they don't agree with
types a message and then deletes it
watches the clock on a station wall tick forward
draws a shape in spilled salt on a kitchen table
backs against a wall when the room fills with people
adjusts a frame on the wall until it's exactly level
crosses the street to avoid passing a specific building
holds an umbrella open indoors and doesn't explain why
sets two cups down and doesn't use either of them
stands at the edge of an empty swimming pool
presses a hand against a cold window
reads the same paragraph six times without moving
returns something that was never borrowed
shuts the curtains in the middle of the afternoon
sits on a fire escape and watches the alley below
checks the lock a final time and then stops checking
leaves the engine running while they think
looks up just before the light changes
eats standing up in a kitchen at midnight
carries flowers to a grave and then forgets to put them down
sits in the back of a moving car staring out the rear window
takes off their shoes before walking into an empty room
goes through a stranger's mail by mistake and reads one line
turns off their phone and puts it face-down on the table
pulls back a curtain to find another curtain behind it
leans against a doorframe without going through it
peels a label off a bottle while listening to someone talk
sits with their back to the room in a crowded cafe
empties a box and then sits inside it
lets a call go to voicemail and then listens to it immediately
crosses their arms the moment someone sits down opposite them
stirs a coffee that's already gone cold
makes the bed before checking if they're leaving
holds a key in their fist so long it leaves a mark
steps over a threshold and then steps back
watches someone pack their things without offering to help
wipes their hands clean on a towel and then holds still
feeds a stray animal and then walks away quickly
stands under a streetlight at the exact moment it turns off
takes the long route home through streets they used to know
opens a window in November and lets the cold in
cuts their hair over the bathroom sink without a mirror
presses their face against a cold pillow in a hot room
picks up a stone from the road and puts it in their pocket
reads graffiti on a wall that was meant for someone else
arrives exactly on time and waits in the car until they're late
writes an address on an envelope and then leaves it blank
folds a map back to where the journey started
watches the news with the sound off
sits at a table with an empty chair across from them
locks a suitcase that has nothing valuable inside
leaves a voicemail that is mostly silence
finishes someone else's abandoned crossword on the subway
drops coins into a fountain with their eyes closed
reads the departures board for a city they've never been to
checks the expiry date on something they bought years ago
holds a door open for someone who isn't there yet
presses send and closes the laptop before it loads
takes off a wedding ring and puts it on a different finger
polishes shoes that aren't going anywhere
puts a kettle on for someone who just left

lines up the bottles in the fridge exactly parallel
slips a note into a book they're returning to a stranger
sits outside a school gate at pickup time, alone
leaves one light on in every room
tucks a blanket around someone who's already asleep
knocks on a door they know won't open
watches a match flicker out rather than striking another
closes a book on the last page without putting it down
stands at the base of a staircase and decides not to go up
wipes a fogged mirror to find only their own face
